about.title: About me
about.first-text: I'm an Automation Specialist focused on optimizing processes, integrating systems, and improving operational efficiency through automation. I build end-to-end automated workflows using Make, Zapier, and n8n, supported by a solid background in web and backend development with JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Java and Spring Boot.
about.second-text: I combine precise analysis, structured thinking, and agile execution to design effective solutions.

product_api.title: Product Management - API Rest
product_api.description: This project is a RESTful API built with Spring Boot, designed for managing products, categories, users, and orders.

medical_app.title: Medical Appointments Manager
medical_app.description: This is a web application developed using Spring Boot that allows patients to register, view available doctors, and schedule or cancel medical appointments.

chat_app.title: Real-Time Chat Application
chat_app.description: Real-time messaging app for public rooms and private chats. Built the backend with Node.js + TypeScript, Express, Socket.IO and the frontend with React + TailwindCSS

attendance_api.title: Employee Attendance System - API Rest
attendance_api.description: This is a RESTful API built with Spring Boot for managing employee attendance within a company.

event_driven_webhook.description: Centralized webhook processor that normalizes incoming events and triggers actions across Google Sheets, Todoist, and HubSpot with Slack notifications.

data_transformation_pipeline.description: Robust system that processes dirty JSON, validates, transforms, and normalizes data, generating clean outputs with error handling, structured logging, and export to multiple formats.

focus_tracker_webhook_service.description: Production-oriented webhook microservice that securely validates incoming events using HMAC signatures, processes focus session data, persists structured logs and events, and integrates with external APIs while handling failures gracefully.

workflows.title: Event-Driven Webhook Processing & Action Orchestration
workflows.shortDescription: Centralized webhook processor that normalizes incoming events and triggers actions across Google Sheets, Todoist, and HubSpot with Slack notifications.
workflows.longDescription: This workflow, built entirely in n8n, processes incoming contact events from multiple sources (forms, applications, platforms), normalizes the data, dynamically routes events to the appropriate system, and sends contextual notifications to Slack for full operational visibility.
workflows.context: Previously, incoming contact information from various sources had to be manually processed, leading to delays, inconsistent formatting, and a lack of visibility into where data was stored or actions taken. This workflow centralizes the process and automates it end-to-end.
workflows.objective: Automate the ingestion, normalization, routing, and notification of contact events to improve accuracy, efficiency, and operational visibility, reducing manual intervention.
flow: Webhook receives incoming contact information (name, email, company, employee size) and source (FORM, APP, PLATFORM).
Edit Fields node normalizes data: emails to lowercase, names capitalized, company and source in uppercase.
Switch node routes events based on source:
FORM: Save contact information to Google Sheets.
APP: Create a task in Todoist. Priority is high if company has more than 1000 employees, medium otherwise.
PLATFORM: Create a new contact in HubSpot.
Slack node sends a notification with details about the new submission and its storage destination.
workflows.result: Reduced manual data processing time, ensured consistent and normalized contact information, improved operational visibility with Slack notifications, and dynamically prioritized tasks in Todoist based on company size.
workflows.imageAlt: Diagram showing the Event-Driven Webhook workflow in n8n with routing and Slack notifications

workflows.title: Lead Intake, Validation & Intelligent Orchestration
workflows.shortDescription: End-to-end lead processing workflow that validates, normalizes, scores, and routes leads across HubSpot, Google Sheets, Gmail, and Slack.
workflows.longDescription: This workflow, built with n8n, automates the complete lifecycle of incoming leads. It validates and normalizes data, detects duplicates, calculates lead scores, and dynamically routes each lead to the appropriate action based on business rules, ensuring efficiency, data quality, and operational visibility.
workflows.context: Incoming leads from different sources were previously processed manually, leading to inconsistent data, duplicate records, delayed responses, and limited visibility for the sales team. Error handling was minimal, making troubleshooting difficult and time-consuming.
workflows.objective: Automate lead ingestion, validation, normalization, scoring, and routing while providing reliable error handling, logging, and real-time notifications to improve sales efficiency and data quality.
flow: Webhook receives incoming lead data from external sources.
Code node validates required fields and data formats; invalid requests receive an immediate error response.
Data is normalized: emails are converted to lowercase, country codes to uppercase, and names to title case.
Google Sheets node checks if the email already exists to prevent duplicate leads.
If a duplicate is detected, the workflow logs the event and returns a duplicate response.
A Code node calculates the lead score based on predefined business rules.
A Switch node routes the lead based on its score:
Cold leads (score < 40): send a confirmation email via Gmail.
Warm leads (score 41–80): create a contact in HubSpot.
Hot leads (score > 80): create a contact in HubSpot and automatically generate a ticket.
The lead is stored in Google Sheets with its lead type and timestamp.
A success response is sent through the webhook and a notification is sent to Slack.
Unhandled errors are captured by an Error Trigger and logged in Google Sheets with execution details.
workflows.result: The workflow reduces manual lead handling, ensures consistent and validated data, prevents duplicates, improves response times for high-value leads, and provides full visibility through structured logging and Slack notifications.
workflows.imageAlt: Diagram of the Lead Intake and Scoring workflow in n8n showing validation, routing, and integrations

workflows.title: Secure Focus Tracker Automation & Workflow Orchestration
workflows.shortDescription: Event-driven automation workflow that validates, normalizes, signs, and routes requests securely between external clients, a signature service, and a focus tracking API.
workflows.longDescription: This workflow, built with n8n, orchestrates secure communication between external systems and backend services through HMAC-based request signing, payload normalization, retry mechanisms, centralized logging, and workflow-level error handling. The automation validates incoming requests, generates cryptographic signatures through a dedicated signature microservice, forwards signed requests to the Focus Tracker API, logs operational events into Notion, and handles failures through dedicated error branches and workflow triggers.
workflows.context: The Focus Tracker ecosystem required a reliable automation layer capable of securely orchestrating communication between multiple services while maintaining operational visibility and resilience. Manual request handling and the lack of centralized monitoring made debugging, validation, and error tracking inefficient and difficult to scale.
workflows.objective: Build a secure and resilient orchestration workflow capable of validating incoming webhook requests, normalizing data, generating HMAC signatures, communicating securely with backend APIs, handling transient failures through retries, and providing centralized operational logging and error management.
flow: Webhook receives incoming focus tracking requests from external clients.
A validation node verifies that all required fields are present and correctly formatted.
Invalid requests immediately trigger an error response and generate operational logs in Notion.
Incoming data is normalized into a standardized internal structure before processing.
The workflow sends the normalized payload to a dedicated Signature Service responsible for generating HMAC signatures.
The Signature Service request includes retry logic with up to three attempts for transient failures.
If the Signature Service becomes unavailable, the workflow logs the failure into Notion and returns an error response through the webhook.
The signed payload is forwarded to the Focus Tracker API using the generated x-signature header.
Successful API responses trigger the creation of operational records in Notion.
A success message is sent to Slack to notify successful workflow execution.
The workflow responds back to the webhook caller with a success response.
Failed API requests generate structured logs in Notion and return descriptive error responses.
An Error Trigger captures unhandled workflow-level failures and stores execution details for operational debugging and monitoring.
workflows.result: The workflow provides a secure and resilient orchestration layer for the Focus Tracker ecosystem, reducing manual intervention, improving operational visibility, enabling secure API communication through HMAC signatures, and ensuring robust error handling through retries, centralized logging, and workflow-level monitoring.
workflows.imageAlt: Diagram of the Secure Focus Tracker Automation workflow in n8n showing validation, HMAC signing, API orchestration, retries, logging, and error handling
